dataset.title: Alaska Permafrost Soils Inventory and Thermokarst Monitoring Database 2024 Update

abstract.para: This database contains data on site, soil stratigraphy, soil physical and chemical properties, Carbon-14 (C14) and stable isotope, and vegetation composition and structure acquired from permafrost soil surveys and thermokarst monitoring sites. The data are from projects that we have conducted, as well as data compiled from numerous other project and reports, that have emphasized the study of the intermediate layer of upper permafrost and the dynamic responses of permafrost to environmental conditions. This 2024 update includes data from our recent National Science Foundation (NSF)-funded project on the upper permafrost. The Access Database has 11 main data tables (tbl_) for site (environmental), soil stratigraphy, soil physical data, soil chemical data, water Oxygen-18 (O18), soil radiocarbon dates, vegetation cover, vegetation structure, study areas, personnel, and project data sources. The Site data includes information of location, observers, geomorphology, topography, hydrology, soil summary characteristics, pH and electrical conductivity (EC), soil classification, and vegetation cover by species. Soil stratigrapy has information on soil texture and ground ice. Soil physical and chemical data includes lab data on bulk density, moisture, carbon, and nitrogen. The database has 42 reference tables (REF_) that have codes and descriptions for variables used in site, soil stratigraphy, and vegetation cover tables. Query tables (qry_) are used to link data tables and reference tables to display data with names instead of codes. In addition to the permafrost soils information, the Site data includes topographic survey control and other monitoring information for repeat monitoring of thermokarst study areas. Some sites have repeat sampling so there can be more than one record per site. As of end of 2024, there were 3412 records in the Site table, including 2728 sites with soils data and 1208 with permafrost data (some sites have multiple records from repeat sampling). This database is a work in progress, is incomplete, and has not undergone complete and universal quality checks, so user should do additional review before using.

intellectualRights.para: This work is dedicated to the public domain under the Creative Commons Universal 1.0 Public Domain Dedication. To view a copy of this dedication, visit https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/.

geographicCoverage.geographicDescription: Boreal and Arctic Alaska and Canadian High Arctic

section.title: Ethical Research Practices
section.para: The research followed community standards for ethic research by having a valid scientific sampling design to sample and describe permafrost soil characteristics. No animals or people were the subject of the research. The analyses and results were reviewed by all the co-authors. Numerous paper presenting the results has been peer-reviewed and accepted by a scientific journal. Data are available to the public. The research has societal value because it addresses the impacts of climate change on arctic and boreal ecosystems. The data are of value to the National Park Service, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, Bureau of Land Management, and the Alaska Department of Natural Resources for their land management decisions on their respective lands.

description.para: Site environmental descriptions (Tbl_Site) included information on observers, location, plot type, topography, geomorphology, soils, hydrology, and vegetation using the ecological land classification approach of Jorgenson et al. (2009, https://irma.nps.gov/DataStore/DownloadFile/148258). Photographs of vegetation and soil cores were taken each site. Data were reviewed after fieldwork for consistency and completeness, and data summarizing soil and vegetation characteristics were added to the site-level data. See ELS_Arctic_Boreal_Site_Soil_Veg_Code_Sheet_2024.pdf for field variables and coding used for collecting site information. See steps below for soil and vegetation methods.

Note, however, that the data have been collected by numerous investigators over many decades using different protocols focused on differing site, soil, and vegetation characteristics. Thus, data collection was inconsistent, and many observations lack complete records.

Soil stratigraphy (Tbl_Soil Stratigraphy) was described from soil plugs, cores, and bank exposures. In unfrozen surface soils, ~30-centimeter (cm) diameter (diam.) soil plugs were extracted with a shovel for subsampling with knives or small corers. For frozen soils, SIPRE corer (7.5 cm diam.) was used and cored to 1–5.2 meter (m) depths. Soil stratigraphy was described according to Natural Resources Conservation Service field sampling methods. Coarse-fragment (>2 millimeter [mm]) percentage was visually estimated. Cryostructures were described according to French and Shur (2010). Peat types were differentiated by dominant plant macrofossils identifiable in the field with a hand lens.

For soil physical properties (Tbl_Soil Physical). Soil samples were obtained every systematically (every ~20 cm) or subjectively from distinctive horizons. For most samples, sample dimensions were measured for volumetric analyses. In the field, determinations were made for wet weights, and soil pH and electrical conductivity were measured in thawed liquids or a saturated paste with a portable meter calibrated daily. In the lab, samples were dried at 60 deg C for ~8 days and weighed for calculating bulk density, gravimetric and volumetric moisture, and excess-ice contents.

For soil chemical properties (Tbl_Soil Chemical). Samples were analyzed by various labs using standard procedures for Total Carbon Nitrogen (TCN) (typically LECO CHN analyzer), pH, electrical conductivity, and extractable cations (K [Potassium], Ca [Calcium], M [Magnesium], Na [Sodium]). For typical procedures, see Ping et al. (2011, https://doi.org/10.1029/2010JG001588)

Vegetation cover was sampled using various methods (or not at all) including point sampling, occular estimates, or simply listing of dominant species. For point sampling, a vertically oriented laser pointer was used to determine species occurrence at 100 points per plot and noted “hits” for the first occurrence of a species at each point. For points with no live plant, litter (dead plant parts) was recorded if present, or bare soil if litter was absent. Surface water was recorded when present above or below live plants. After point sampling, the plots were examined for additional species and a cover of 0.1% was assigned to species that were present but not recorded by the point-sampling. The total number of hits of each species was summed to determine percent cover. For occular estimates, the percent cover was visually estimated for each taxa. For many sites, a simple list of the dominant plant taxa (typically 5 to 10 taxa) were noted. Vascular taxa were identified to species level, when possible, while non-vascular plants were identified to species only for dominant taxa. During data management, the percent cover by site and year were tabulated , assigned final taxonomic determinations for each species, and aggregated problematic (inconsistent identification) and trace species into higher taxonomic or growth form levels.

Sampling locations mostly were established using a multi-level gradient-oriented sampling design. First study areas were chosen along a climatic and physiographic gradients to represent a range of environmental conditions across permafrost-affected landscapes. Within a study area, most sampling was done along an elevational transect with the transect oriented to cross a diversity of environmental conditions. Along the transect, the sites were stratified by ecotype (obviously different environmental conditions). Often additional sites were sampled off transect to include ecotypes not encountered along the transect. When possible ecotypes were replicated. For data compiled from other projects, we assume the sampling used targeted sampling. For monitoring change and intensive thermokarst study area, sampling was repeated over 2 to 5 years: in one study area (Jago) sampling was repeated during five separate years.

samplingDescription.para: Data in tbl_Site were collected using methods by the National Soil Survey Center (V2.0 Schoeneberger et al. 2002) and Jorgenson et al. (2009, NPS/ARCN/NRTR—2009/270). Data in tbl_Soil_Stratigraphy follow methods in Schoeneberger (NCCS 2002), Ping et al. (2013, Soil Horizons), and French and Shur (2010, Earth-Science Reviews), but primarily focus on texture and ice structures important to engineering geology. Data in tbl_Soil_Physical were collected and processed using methods in Jorgenson et al. (2015, JGR Earth Surface). Data for individual chemical analyses in tbl_Soil_Chemical are provided in metadata associated with table, with most methods follow those in Ping et al. (2001, JGR). Methods for tbl_Soil_C14 are listed in table. Data for tbl_Veg_Cover were collected through either point sampling (see Jorgenson et al. 2015, JGR Earth Surface) or ocular estimates within variable sized plots.

Jorgenson, M. T., J. E. Roth, P. F. Miller, M. J. Macander, M. S. Duffy, A. F. Wells, G. V. Frost, and E. R. Pullman. An ecological land survey and landcover map of the Arctic Network. Natural Resource Technical Report NPS/ARCN/NRTR—2009/270. National Park Service, Fort Collins, Colorado.

Ping, C.-L., G. J. Michaelson, L. Guo, M. T. Jorgenson, M. Kanevskiy, Y. Shur, F. Dou, and J. Liang (2011), Soil carbon and material fluxes across the eroding Alaska Beaufort Sea coastline, J. Geophys. Res., 116, G02004, doi:10.1029/2010JG001588.
Schoeneberger, P & Wysocki, D & Benham, E & Broderson, W. (2002). Field Book for Describing and Sampling Soils, Version 2.0. Natural Resources Conservation Service.
French, Hugh, and Yuri Shur. "The principles of cryostratigraphy." Earth-Science Reviews 101.3-4 (2010): 190-206.

project.title: Collaborative Research: The Transition Zone of Upper Permafrost: The Frontline for Permafrost Changes across Climate and Landscape Gradients

award.title: Collaborative Research: The Transition Zone of Upper Permafrost: The Frontline for Permafrost Changes across Climate and Landscape Gradients

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for O18 measurements

attribute.attributeDefinition: FieldID for sample
ID for site
LabID for sample
Name of lab that performed analysis
Short name of project
Code for study area
Name of sample collector
Year sample taken
Sample Depth in cm
Date results reported by lab
Hydrogen-2 isotope delta (d) values (0/00) in relation to Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (VSMOW)
Standard deviation of Hydrogen isotope delta (d) values (0/00) in relation to Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (VSMOW)
Oxygen-18 isotope delta (d) values (0/00) in relation to Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (VSMOW)
Standard deviation of Oxygen-18 isotope delta (d) values (0/00) in relation to Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (VSMOW)
Type of sample medium: precip (PRC), surface water (SW), groundwater (GW), permafrost (PF)
Notes

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for vegetation cover by plant structure

attribute.attributeDefinition: ID
Concatenated Unique ID field
Needle Leaf Tree (>3m)(%cov)
Needle Leaf Tree(>3m)(crwn class)
Needleleaf Tr (>3m)(size:s=seedling,a=sampling,p=pole,t=timber,L)
Broadleaf Tree (>3m)(%cov)
Broadleaf Tree (>3m)(crwn class)
Broadleaf Tr (>3m)
Dwarf Broadleaf (<3m)(%cov)
Dwarf Broadleaf Tree (<3m)(crwn class)
Dwarf Needleleaf (<3m)(%cov)
Dwarf Needleleaf (<3m)(crwn class)
Standing Dead tree (%cov)
Tall Shrub (>1.5m)(%cov)
Low Shrub (0.2-1.5 m)(%cov)
Dwarf Shrub (<0.2m)
Graminoids % Cov
Whole Tussock %Cov
Forb %Cov
Moss %Cov
Lichen %Cov
Litter %Cov
Litter Alone %Cov
Bare Soil %Cov includes rock
Water %Cov
Notes

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for vegetation cover by species

attribute.attributeDefinition: SiteID and year concatinated
Unique site or plot ID
SiteID used in field
Date and time
Year of sampling
Code for study area
Project Name
Sampling period, e.g., 1, 2,3 when repeated monitoring
siteID and pointID
point sampling interval, sequential number
Total number of layers at sampling point, integer
layer at sampling point, integer
Relative position of layer
Type of hit at a point: This is used to make different types of sampling compatable.

codeDefinition.definition: trace when observed but not hit

attribute.attributeDefinition: 6 letter species code used in field, based on Hulten, Viereck and Little
Final 6 letter species code after review and update on taxonomy
6 letter species code after aggregation of problem taxa
Visual estimate of percent cover for each species, field
Visual estimate of percent cover, final, adjusted to add to 100+
Vegetation sampling method: O=occular estimate, P=point sampling, L-line intercept, I=incidental for area collections
Whether a voucher specimen was collected (yes/no), otherwise S=specimen, P=Photo
Species ID of voucher specimen
Person performing ID on voucher specimen
Synonym for voucher specimen
Notes from VoucherID Person
Notes of species ID problem
field notes
office notes to document decisions
Abundance category: Used for filtering out trace species in floristic analysis
Variable used for identifying which rows to be used for summing cover data by plot, mainly for summing point sampling data. Data used when cell=CovPct

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table with study area central coordinates

attribute.attributeDefinition: Auto number
Project that initiated the study area
study area comprising a group of related sampling sites
Person actively managing the site or who did the most sampling at the location
Latitude, decimal degrees
Longitude, decimal degrees
Elevation, WGS84
Arctic or Boreal
Based on Nowacki et al. 2001, Ecoregions of Alaska
Type of site
Number of sites sampled within study area
Number of sites with soils data from the active layer
Number of sites with surface/deep soil temps
Number of water level stations within study area
Number of time-lapse cameras within study area
Number of permafrost cores sampled within study area
Number of sites with air temperature sensors
Whether a toposurvey transect has been done for a study area: yes/no
Whether a resistivity transect has been done for a study area: yes/no/planned
Years of observations at study area
Monitoring status: active, inactive, abandoned, proposed
Fire status for study area: unburned, burned, mixed burned-unburned
Priority for sustained monitoring at study area
Notes

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for soil characteristics by strata

attribute.attributeDefinition: Unique ID for site/plot and soil horizon using topdepth of layer, some Horizons may need to be split for sampleID linkage
Unique SiteID concatonated with year
Unique ID for site/plot and pedon
Field ID used for site/plot and pedon
Project Name from Site table
study area code or name
Name of observer
Year of sampling
Sequential number for each horizon within the soil profile as originally described
Depth (cm) to top of horizon, mm level
Depth (cm) to bottom of horizon, mm level
Mid depth (m) of layer: calculated field (DepthTop + DepthBot)/200
Thickness of layer (cm), Calculated field: Bottom Depth minus Top Depth
0-100, 100-200, 200+, etc., used to sum data by 1-m increments (cm)
Geomorphic stratigraphic unit, see reference table REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv, nd=not determined
Lithofacies code that differentiates soil texture and bedding structure together, see reference table REF_Soil_Lithofacies_2024.csv
Horizon Boundary NRCS Distinctness and Topography, nd=not determined
NRCS horizon designation, primary and secondary if turbated, see reference table REF_Soil_Horizon_2024.csv, nd=not determined
NRCS horizon designation, primary and secondary if turbated, see reference table REF_Soil_Horizon_2024.csv, nd=not determined
Aggregation and simplification of horizons for analysis
Special grouping of horizons for analysis, for example grouping horizons above ice wedges (abvwdg, icewdg, nxtwdg)
Texture as originally described, crs text+Primary texture of fine material, + secon text
Texture standardized to NRCS system, combined coarse and prim and secondary fine textures, see REF_Soil_Texture
Fine portion of texture, see reference table REF_Soil_Texture_2024.csv
Secondary texture, see reference table REF_Soil_Texture_2024.csv
Coarse fragment modifier for primary texture, see reference table REF_Soil_Coarse_Texture_2024.csv
United Soil Classification System
Texture, Aggregated for analysis, see SoilTxtMin40 on field coding sheet
Texture Aggregated 2: O-organic, OM-organic mineral, M-mineral
TextAgg1 with IceAgg1 combined, see soil texture and soil ice structure codes
Further aggregation of textAgg1 and IceAgg2 to reduce classes
texture and Horizon Aggregated Lvl1
texture and Horizon Aggregated Lvl2
Fragment percent volume occupied by fragments > 2 mm
Fragment size (mm), maximum
Fragment shape, see reference table REF_Soil_Frag_Shape_2024.csv
Peat type described in field, see reference table REF_Soil_Peat_Type_2024.csv
Peat type final, see reference table REF_Soil_Peat_Type_2024.csv
Peat type aggregated 1-for analysis, see PeatType on field coding sheet
Peat type aggregated 2-narrowly for analysis
Origin of peat relative to thermokarst or fire: PostTK, PreTK, RepTK (earlier episode),PostFire,PreFire,RepFire
Van Post scale or short note
Color, munsell name or general field name
Color, Munsell value/chroma/hue
Mottle color, Munsell value/chroma/hue or common name
Color moisture state, normally moist, see reference table REF_soil_colour_2024.csv/REF_Site_Soil_Moisture_2024.csv
Mottle quantity, contrast, size, see reference table REF_Soil_Mottle_QCS_2024.csv
Redoximorphic status, see reference table REF_Soil_Redox_Type_2024.csv
Structure type, size, grade, see reference table REF_Soil_Structure_Type_2024.csv
Consistency, Rupture resistance, see reference table REF_Soil_Consistency_Rupture_Resist_Block_2024.csv
Plasticity, see reference table REF_Soil_Consistency_Stickiness_Plasticity_2024.csv
Stickiness, see reference table
Roots size and quantity, see reference codes
Frost status, F=frozen or U=unfrozen at time of sampling
Cryostratigraphic Unit, Field interpretation of Genesis of permafrost types, see REF_Cryostratigraphy
Cryostratigraphic Unit, Final interpretation of Genesis of permafrost types, see reference table REF_Cryostratigraphy
Cryostratigraphic Unit, Aggregated to primary types for analysis, See REF_Cryostratigraphic_Units or field code sheet
Percent visible ice, estimated
Ice lens maximum size in mm
Ice Structure in field, See ice classification, includes primary, secondary, and tertiary properties
Ice structure, primary, see REF_Ice_Structure
Ice structure, secondary, see ice classes but coding is free form
Shape (Round, Tubular, Irregular, None, Unknown) and size class of bubbles (very fine, fine, medium, large, very large)
Lenght (mm) of bubble tubes, or width of round bubbles
Ice structures Aggregation 1 (10-20 classes) for analyses, See Ref_Soil_Ice_Structure or field coding sheet
Ice structures Aggregation 2 (5-10 classes) for analyses
pH by saturated paste
electrical conductivity (uS/cm) by saturated paste
Original field notes on layer descriptions
Office notes on data problems and changes

Type of sampling: m=meas, s=SplitDup (for layers that are split and duplicated across 100 and 200 cm boundaries, or for soil samples), e=Extended es=Ext-short (<50 cm) to get to full meter increment, el=Ext-long (>50 cm) to get to full meter increment
Type of physical sample data used for gap filling: Meas, Ave, AbvBlw, Nearby
Type of chem sample data used for gap filling: Meas (measured), Ave (average for similar texture/ice), Sub_AbvBlw (from layer above or below), Sub_Nearby (from similar layer in nearby profile)
Sample ID for lab sample data for moisture, bulk density
Sample ID for lab sample for chemistry data such as carbon, texture, nutrients
Sample ID for lab sample for radiocarbon dating
Sample ID for lab sample for O18/H
Sample ID to assign BD for thawed material, actual for active layer, average thawed BD for frozen layers
Broad ecological stages of soil/peat development
Density for layer (g/cc), Derived from qry_soil-strat-Phys-chem after sample substitution, not adjusted for crs Frag_pct
Moisture (%vol) for layer, Derived from qry_soil-strat-Phys-chem after sample substitution, adjusted for crs Frag_pct
Total Carbon (%) for layer, Derived from qry_soil-strat-Phys-chem after sample substitution, adjusted for crs Frag_pct
Total Nitrogen (%) for layer, Derived from qry_soil-strat-Phys-chem after sample substitution, adjusted for crs Frag_pct
Organic Carbon (%) for layer, Derived from qry_soil-strat-Phys-chem after sample substitution, adjusted for crs Frag_pct
Organic Carbon Density (g/cc) for layer: Carbon density calculated from measured and substituted data, gm/cc
OC-stock (kg/m2) calculated for layer, see calculation query
Total Carbon Density (gm/cc) for layer: Carbon density calculated from measured and substituted data,
Total carbon-stock calculated for layer, see calculation query, kg/m2
Depth of water equivalent for layer (m of water equivalent per depth of layer), using crsFrag adjusted moisture
Calculated thaw strain (fraction) based of frozen density and thawed density
Calculated thaw settlement based on thaw strain and thickness

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for soil moisture and density measurements

attribute.attributeDefinition: Unique ID for sample, final, concatonate StudyArea-SiteID-TopDepth
Unique ID for sample, field or original
ID used by lab for sample
SiteID matching site table
SiteID with year concatenated
Year, integer
Study area, sampling group
Person(s) doing sampling or processing samples
Top depth of sample from ground surface, cm
Bottom depth of sample, cm
Mid depth of sample, m below surface, negative number
Sample length 1, mm
Sample length 2, mm
Sample length 3, mm
Average of lengths, mm
Diameter, mm
Circumference 1, mm
Circumference 2, mm
Circumference 3, mm
Circumference Average, mm
Volume (cm3), calculated from lengths, diam., or circ., depending on sample type
Adjusted Volume (cm3), when volume if poor, for example when Moist_volpct greater than 100%
Fraction for adjusting volume, 1 when no adjustment
Sample type, Ave-class average (for gap filling), Vol-volumetric, Grav=gravimetric, RC-C14, O18, water, calib=meter calibration, ice, missing
Sample tool used (knife, ring, hand-drill, SIPRE)
Frozen or Unfrozen
ID of lab container, or "bag" if no unique ID
Weight of Container, gm
Field Weight of Wet Soil + Container Wt, gm; usually the value used for calculations
Field Weight of Wet Soil after decanting of excess water, used for calculating excess ice
Lab Weight of Wet Soil + Container Wt, gm, used to check for loss of sample during transport
Weight of Dry Soil + Container Wt, gm

valueURI.label: soil dry weight and container weight

attribute.attributeDefinition: Weight of wet soil after subtracting container wt, gm
Weight of dry soil after subtracting container wt, gm
Weight of water loss, or weight change, gm
Dry or Bulk Density, gm/cm3
Dry or Bulk Density, gm/cm3 after adjustment if coarse fraction removed
Meas-measured or Est-Estimated, estimates from regression equations or substitution
Moisture (water) content, gravimetric< %
Moisture (water) content, volumetric, %
Moisture (water) content, volumetric, %, after adjustment for removed coarse fragments
Meas=measured, Est=estimated through regression or substitution
Moisture (water) content, gravimetric< %, after decanting excess water after thawing
Ice volume % = moisture-%vol X 1.09
Weight of excess water decanted from sample
Excess Ice in Percent, volume of ice in excess of pore space
Method for determining excess ice: M-measured (water decanted from sample), C=calculated based on density ratio (bulk to dry)

codeDefinition.definition: calculated based on density ratio (bulk to dry)

attribute.attributeDefinition: field pH measurement in saturated paste, usually with Oakton PCSTestr 35 multi parameter field probe
field EC measurement in saturated paste, microS/cm, usually with Oakton PCSTestr 35 multi parameter field probe
Texture of sample in field
Texture, aggregated for plotting broad groups: M, O, OM
Percent wt of gravel >2 mm fraction
Percent wt of sand fractions
Percent wt of silt fractions
Percent wt of clay fractions
Note
Note
Note
Data quality, good overall, or : Good, Fair, Poor, Outlier. list problems with particular parameters in NotesOff field
Lab samples where sent or analyzed
Lab request for Gravel Percent, y or n
Lab request for Particle size analysis, y or n
Lab request for Loss on Ignition, y or n
Lab request for Total Carbon Nitrogen, y or n
Lab request for CaCO3 equivalent, y or n
Lab request for O18, y or n
Lab request for C14, y or n
other analyses requested

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for soil chemical measurements

attribute.attributeDefinition: Final sample ID
Field sample ID
Lab sample ID, analyses done by many different labs
Site ID
Location code for study area
Project Name from Site table
Top depth of sample, cm
Bottom depth of sample, cm
Mid-depth of sample, m, expressed as negative value below surface
Type of sample: grab, sipre core, cylinder
F-frozen, or U-unfrozen
Lab method for total carbon analysis
Lab or calculation method for Organic carbon determination
Loss on Ignition, %wt
Total Carbon, % wt, usually determined with LECO CN analyzer
Total Nitrogen, % wt, , usually determined with LECO CN analyzer
Organic Carbon, %wt, usually determined by LECO CN analyzer for acidic soils, or after acidic fumigation for alkaline soils, or by Total carbon minus Inorganic carbon
Calcium Carbonate equivalent results, %wt, used for inorganic carbon determination
Inorganic Carbon, %wt, calculated from CaCO3-equiv%, Step 1 CaCO3 X 0.12 to get the CO3-C
C to N ratio, %
Cation Exchange Concentration, cmol/kg
Base Saturation, %
"Potassium, millequivalents/100g, usually ICP spectroscopy on neutral 1 N ammonium acetate extracts of air‐dried samples"
Calcium, millequivalents/100g, usually ICP spectroscopy on neutral 1 N ammonium
Magnesium, millequivalents/100g, usually ICP spectroscopy on neutral 1 N ammonium
Sodium, millequivalents/100g, usually ICP spectroscopy on neutral 1 N ammonium
Ammonium, mg/kg, usually by specific ion electrode
Nitrate, mg/kg, usually by specific ion electrode
Total Phosphorus, mg/kg, usually by Olsen Phosphorus for alkaline soils, Bray Phosphorus for acidic soils
Total Sulfur, %
Notes
Data quality, good overall, Good, Fair, Poor, Outlier. Describe issue with particular parameter in Notes field

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for C14 measurements

attribute.attributeDefinition: Final Sample identifier
Field sample identifier
Lab Sample identifier
SiteID
Name of project
Laboratory that performed analysis
Receipt number from lab
Date sampled
Date sent to lab
Date reported by lab
Top Depth of sample, cm
Bottom Depth of sample, cm
Mid depth of sample, negative for below surface, m
kind of material
type of sample
description of sample
preprocessing of sample
% carbon, if available
Delta C-13 isotopic ratios comparing Carbon-13 to the more common Carbon-12
Estimated or measured source of data
Fraction modern, %
Standard Error of Fraction Modern
14C radiocarbon age
Standard error of 14C age
Text description for out of range samples
d14C represents the per mille depletion in sample carbon 14 prior to isotopic fractionation correction
D14C-Error
Calibrated calendar year age. 1 sigma
Standard error of calibrated age, 1 sigma

valueURI.label: 14C mean calibrated age one standard error

attribute.attributeDefinition: Calibrated calendar year age. 2 sigma
Standard error of calibrated age, 2 sigma

valueURI.label: 14C mean calibrated age two standard errors

attribute.attributeDefinition: Method used for age calibration
Median probability of calibrated age
Interpretation of geomorphic stage/process
Notes
Interpretation of ecological stage, ecotype

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table for site environmental information

attribute.attributeDefinition: Concatenated field using Site ID and Year
Site ID, often concatenated field using Project Code and Plot ID
Short SiteID for site specific maps
Original field ID
Project short name
Date of field work
Time, 24hr
Year of project
Observer for site environmental information, last name
Observer for vegetation data, last name
Observer for soil data, last name
Observer for water data, last name
Observer for datalogger data, last name
last name of other observers
Biome: A=Arctic, B=Boreal, M=Maritime Northern, O=Boreal Oceanic
Ecoregion based on Nowacki et al. 2001
Name of nearby geographic landmark from USGS topo map
Two to Four letter study area identification code
Latitude in WGS84, decimal degrees
Longitude in WGS84, decimal degrees
Elevation (m), Ellipsoid Ht WGS84, typical of raw gps data, no data=9999
Latitude, NAD83(2011), units decimal degrees, used for special map positioning
Longitude, NAD83(2011), units decimal degrees, used for special map positioning
Elevation (m), Orthometric Ht NAVD88, typically derived from DPGS data and leveling
Method for determining elevation: RecrGPS, DGPS, Level, Est
List of type of data collected at site: perm-permafrost, soil=active layer soil, veg=vegetation, wat=water, dlog=datalogger, toposurv=topographic-geodetic, photo=just photos
Plot size, radius or Length X Width in meters
Plot sample or repeat years, sequence times the plot has been sampled. Used to identify initial and repeat samples
Plot photos taken, y,n, or number of photos
SiteId for Veg plot
SiteID for soil sampling
Station ID for water level measurement
Station ID for datalogger
Experimental treatment or grouping factor
Aggregated treatments for analysis
Physiography, linked to reference codes in REF_Site_PhysiographyCodes_2024.csv
Slope (degrees), 999 for no data
Aspect (degrees), if slope = 0, aspect also = 0 (not north), 999 for no data
Geomorphic or terrain Unit Field call, linked to reference table codes in REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv
Geomorphic or terrain Unit Final, linked to reference table codes in REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv
Alternative geomorph class, when they are cutpoint problems, linked to reference table codes in REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv
Subsurface geomorphic unit, linked to reference table codes in REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv
Subsurface geomorphic unit, see REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv
Geomorphic Units combined surface and subsurface, see REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv
Dominant geomorphic unit in multi-layered system that best represents site characteristics, see final revised codes in REF_Site_Geomorphic_Units_2024.csv
Geomorphic unit aggregated to generalize classes, similar to generalized geology
Macrotopography, linked to reference table codes REF_Site_Topo_Macrotopography_2024.csv
Microtopography, linked to reference table codes REF_Site_Topo_Microtopography_2024.csv
measurement of typical height range of microtopographic features within plot in cm, 999 for no data
Depth of depression in meters, used for depth of thermokarst feature estimated in field or measured from DEM
Thermokarst type, linked reference table codes REF_Site_Thermokarst_Types_2024.csv
Thermokarst or degradation stage, see reference table REF_Site_Thermokarst_Stage_2024.csv
Thermokarst or degradation stage Alternative Call, see reference table, REF_Site_Thermokarst_Stage_2024.csv
% cover of surface frags, 999 for no data
% cover of active partially vegetated frost boils, 999 for no data
Water depth above or below the soil surface, cm, 999 if no data
Water depth above or below the soil surface (A/B/U), used to check WaterDep value
soil saturated in upper 30 cm. yes/no/unknown
National Wetlands inventory hydrology regime, see REF_Site_NWIWaterRegimeCodes_2024.csv
pH measured in ground water, where present, 999 if no data
EC (uS/cm) measured in ground water, where present; 999 if no data
pH measure in saturated soil slurry, usually when ground water not present, 0 if no data
soil pH measured from 30cm below soil surface, 0 if no data
Soil pH at 10 cm - Soil pH at 40 cm, 0 if no data
EC (uS/cm) measure in saturated soil slurry, usually when ground water not present, 0 if no data
Water pH or soil slurry pH, water used preferentially when available, 0 if no data
Water EC (uS/cm) or soil slurry EC, water used preferentially when available, uS/cm, 0 if no data
Site Chemistry, codes also in REF_Site_Chemistry_2024.csv
Depth (cm) to top of first layer with mottles, enter 999 for no data
Depth (cm) to top of first layer with full gleying, enter 999 for no data
Soil drainage class, linked to reference table codes REF_Site_Soil_Drainage_2024.csv, nd=no data
Soil Moisture class, linked to reference table codes REF_Site_Soil_Moisture_2024.csv, nd=no data
Is it a hydric soil (yes/no/unknown) using NRCS hydric soil criteria
Thickness (cm) of surface organic layer, 999=no data
Cumulative thickness (cm) of surface and buried organic layer, use 999 when no data
Thickness (cm) of new peat formed after thermokarst, 999 if no data
Dominant mineral texture in top 40 cm, linked to reference table REF_Site_Soil_TextDom_2024.csv
Dominant overall texture in top 40 cm, linked to reference table REF_Site_Soil_TextDom_2024.csv, nd=no data
Depth (cm) of surface wind blown silt
Depth (cm) to rocks, when rocks are >15% volume, use 999 for no data
Thaw depth (cm), 999=no data
Depth to bottom of seasonal or permafrost, 999=no data
Cryoturbation observed in soil profile: P-present or A-absent, U=Unknown
Permafrost present (either proven or highly likely): P-present or A-absent, U=Unknown
Depth (cm) to top of wedge ice, 999 if no data
Depth (cm) to maximum soil observation (cm), includes frost probe depth, 9999 if no data
Soil stratigraphy that was described see either REF_Soil_Cryostratigraphic_Unit_2024.csv for code definitions or y/n (yes/no) for whether soil stratigraphy was described
Soil samples were collected: y/n
method by which soil/environmental variables assessed, see REF_Site_SoilMethod_2024.csv for complete list, nd=no data
Soil taxonomy code used in the field or time of survey, see REF_Site_Soil_Class_2003_2024.csv for codes, NRCS class, nd=no data
NRCS Soil taxonomy final, see REF_Site_Soil_Class_2003_2024.csv for code list, nd=no data
Soil taxonomy Edition used for final, see REF_Site_Soil_Class_2003_2024.csv for code list, nd=no data
Soil landscape combined physiography and dominant texture, nd=no data
Vegetation class Field call, Viereck level 4, linked to reference table codes REF_Site_VegCode4_2024.csv, ND=not determined
Vegetation class Final, Viereck level 4, linked to reference table codes REF_Site_VegCode4_2024.csv, ND=not determined
Viereck Veg class 4 field alternative call, REF_Site_VegCode4_2024.csv, nd=no data
Ecotype Vegetation Structure, linked to reference table codes Ref_Site_Veg_EcoStructure_2024.csv, nd=no data
Listing of dominant plant species, codes use 3 letters of genus and 3 of species, nd=no data
Floristic Class from analysis, nd=no data
Method used to estimate plant cover, code list contained in REF_Site_Veg_Method_2024.csv

codeDefinition.definition: point sampling on grid or line-single hit

attribute.attributeDefinition: Quality or completeness of species listing: P=partial (often 10-20); D=dominants (often 3-8), C=complete (or nearly so), N=None, nd=no data
Disturbance class, natural or human induced, linked to REF_Site_DisturbClass_2024.csv
Year of Disturbance or onset of change, 0=no data
Years of recovery, or interval, after disturbance between disturbance date and sampling date, , no data=0
Ecotype code assigned in field, concatonate Physiog, TextDom, SoilMoist, SiteChem, VegStruct, no data=nd
Aggregated ecotypes (post data- analysis), no data=nd
COE wetland determination: y, n, u
Field Notes
Office Notes
Mean Annual Air Temperature (deg. C), WS=weather station, PRISM=PRISM climate data, no data=99
Mean annual precipitation (mm), WS=weather station, PRISM=PRISM climate data, no data=999
Method for determining MAAT: WS=weather station, P=PRISM model
Environmental (non-geomorphology) data completeness review completed: Y or N
Soil data completeness review performed: Y or N
Vegetation and ecotype data completeness review performed: Y or N
Origin of the data: organization, company, institute or individual, See Tbl_Project_Data_Sources_2024.csv for institutions
Outlier based on evaluation of central tendencies of Environmental Variables; Y (yes) or N (no), assume N unless specifically determined
Outlier based on evaluation of central tendencies of Veg composition; Y (yes) or N (no), assume N unless specifically determined
Reports and publications using data
Total water storage, depth equivalent (m), usually 0-2 m depth increment
Total carbon stock (kg/m2), usually for 0-2 m depth increment
Depth increments (m) used for summing carbon and water stocks
Carbon stock in new peat formed post thermokarst (kg/m2)
Address path for folder with site photos/info. Includes SiteID
Lists types of information available: photos, chart, table, drawings, none
Date folder and data added to website

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table listing sources of project data

attribute.attributeDefinition: Short Name for Project
Title for project
Leader of project
Organization or institution
Organization or institution
Organization or institution
Contact info for project leader
Email for project leader
Date range data were collected
General location or region where data were collected
General description of dataset
Citation of data source or publication using data
Citation of data source or publication using data
Citation of data source or publication using data
Citation of data source or publication using data

dataTable.entityDescription: Data table listing personnel involved in data collection

attribute.attributeDefinition: autonumber
Persons initials
Person's name
Persons role: PI, scientist, tech
Organization
Organization address

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for vegetation taxonomy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Six letter code for species using 3 first letters of genus and first 3 letters of species, sometimes with additional letters for subspecies
Latin name of species using old taxonomy: vascular-primarily Hulten, woody- mostly Viereck&Little, various for mosses and lichens including USDA plants
Latin name of species, current synonomy using Flora of Alaska provisional checklist 2020; FNA Mosses Vol27-28; NorthAmer Lichens 2016-V21
Source for current synonymy
Family name, Hulten
Family name, Flora of Alaska
Genus name
Species name
Name for subspecies or variety
Lifeform (growth form)
Authorities used to describe species
Vascular or nonvascular
Checkbox for when only name to genus
Checkbox for when only name to lifeform
Checkbox for when code is listed on field coding sheet
If the species has a special status based on the references consulted (AKNHP, BLM, Forest Service)

AKNHP uses state and global rankings calculated using the NatureServe Rank Calculator . State rankings are for Alaska. The Forest Service simply states whether a species is "Sensitive." See file "Ranking_Definitions" in ELS_Protocols_Longterm_Data
Reference where the ranking was listed
Growth from level 2, more detailed

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for aggregated texture classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for Texture Aggregated
Name for Texture Aggregated
Description
Used in Database=Y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil texture classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NRCS soil texture class
Short name
Name for NRCS soil texture class
Equivalent Unified Soil Classification
Type of texture and modifiers
Used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil structure classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NRCS structure type
Name for NRCS structure type

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil structure size classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NRCS structure size
Name for NRCS structure size

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil structure grade classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NRCS structure grade
Name for NRCS structure grade

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil redox classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NRCS redoximorphic type
Name for NRCS redoximorphic type

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for peat classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for peat type
Name for peat type
Aggregated Peat Types

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil mottle classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for mode quantity, contrast, size
Name for mode quantity, contrast, size
Description for mode quantity, contrast, size

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil lithofacies classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for soil lithofacies
Name for soil lithofacies
Used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil ice structure classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for Ice Structure
Name for Ice Structure
Description for Ice Structure
Used in database for Primary IceStruct=y
Used in database for IceStructAgg=y
Note

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil horizon suffix classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code used to describe subclasses of soil horizons, using NRCS methods
title for horizon suffixes
Description_Criteria

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil horizon classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: autonumber
Major horizon designator based on NRCS method
Description of horizon

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil fragment size classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for coarse fragment size
Name for coarse fragment size

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil fragment shape classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for coarse fragment shape
Name for coarse fragment shape
Name for coarse fragment shape

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil cryostratigraphic unit classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for permafrost type
Code for permafrost type, Kanevskiy
Name for permafrost type
Description for permafrost type
Used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil consistency classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code describing soil stickiness and plasticity
Title for stickiness and plasticity
Soil consistency class

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil rupture resistance classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NRCS rupture resistance and consistency class
Name for NRCS rupture resistance class
Code for NRCS consistency class

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil color classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Munsel Color code
Munsell Color Name

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil coarse texture classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code describing coarse fragment content and size
Name for coarse fragment content and size

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil boundary topography classes used in Table_Soil_Stratigraphy

attribute.attributeDefinition: NRCS code for boundary distinctness and topography
NRCS name for boundary distinctness and topography
Description

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for vegetation classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for vegetation type in Alaska Vegetation Classification, Viereck et al. 1980
Name for vegetation type in Alaska Vegetation Classification
Code and Name for vegetation type in Alaska Vegetation Classification
Ecotype vegetation structure
Short Name in AVC
Hierarchical grouping in AVC
Code for Level 1 grouping in AVC hierarchy
Code for Level 2 grouping in AVC hierarchy
Code for Level 3 grouping in AVC hierarchy
Code for Level 4 grouping in AVC hierarchy
Description in AVC
Common or used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for vegetation methods used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Vegetation method code
Vegetation method name

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for vegetation ecological structure classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Ecostructure Code
Ecostructure name
Description
Common or used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for microtopography classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for microtopography class
Name for microtopography class
Common or used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for macrotopography classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for Macrotopography Class
Name for Macrotopography Class
Description for Macrotopography Class
Common or used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for thermokarst stages used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for thermokarst or degradation stage
Name for thermokarst or degradation stage
Class Description

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for thermokarst types used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Thermokarst type code
Thermokarst type Name
Dominant type of ice
Common or used in database=y
Hierarchical order of classes

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil sampling method used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for Sample Method
Description of Sample Method

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil dominant texture used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for Dominant mineral/texture
Name for Dominant mineral/texture class
Definition for Dominant mineral/texture class

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil moisture class used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for soil moisture class
Name for soil moisture class

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for soil drainage class used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for drainage class
Name/description for drainage class

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for NRCS soil class used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NRCS soil class
Name for NRCS soil class 2003
Name for NRCS soil class 2010
diagnostic characteristics for soil class

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for physiography class used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for physiography class
Name for Physiography class

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for NWI wetland water regime class used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for NWI class
NWI class name

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for geomorphic unit class used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Alpha code for geomorphic unit
Name of geomorphic unit
Hierarchical group for unit
Common or used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for ecotype Level 8 classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Ecotype code
ecotype name
Common or used in database=y

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for ecotype Level 12 classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code for ecotype
Ecotype name
Physiography portion of name
Soil texture portion of name
Moisture portion of name
Chemistry portion of name
Vegetation structure portion of name

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for disturbance classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: Text code for disturbance class
Short name for distubance class
Level in classification hierarchy

dataTable.entityDescription: Reference table with codes and names for site chemistry classes used in Table_Site

attribute.attributeDefinition: One letter code for Site Chemistry
Full Site Chemistry Names
Site Chemistry definitions

otherEntity.entityDescription: Field coding sheet used in ecological land classification for site and soil descriptions
Listing and description of data tables in the permafrost soils data package
Figure illustration linkages among data and reference tables in permafrost soils database
Metadata for fields in the permafrost soils database output from Access database
Access database for the Alaska permafrost soils inventory and thermokarst monitoring database 2024 version
